name: Penates
description: The Penates were among the most important household deities in ancient Roman religion. They were protectors of the pantry and, by extension, the entire household, ensuring the family's well-being and prosperity. Unlike the Lares, who were tied to a specific location, the Penates accompanied the family wherever they moved, symbolizing the continuation of the family's hearth and traditions. Originating as deities of the storeroom, their worship evolved to encompass the broader concept of the home and family's fortunes. Each family had their own individual Penates, often represented as small statues or figurines kept within the household shrine (lararium). The state also had public Penates, whose sanctuary was located in Rome and were considered protectors of the Roman state itself. Neglecting the Penates was believed to bring misfortune, emphasizing the importance of maintaining their veneration through offerings and rituals. They represented the core values of Roman society: family, tradition, and stability. The Penates were essential for maintaining peace and prosperity within the home and the state, linking the domestic sphere to the civic.
subtitle: Guardians of the Roman Household

keyMyths.title: Aeneas and the Penates
keyMyths.description: Aeneas carried the Penates of Troy with him to Italy, establishing them as protectors of the Roman people. This linked the Roman state to the Trojan lineage and gave divine sanction to Roman expansion.

keyMyths.title: Public Penates of Rome
keyMyths.description: The public Penates were kept in a temple in Rome and were believed to protect the entire state. Their worship was overseen by state officials and was crucial for ensuring the safety and prosperity of Rome.

keyMyths.title: Household Worship of the Penates
keyMyths.description: Roman families maintained a small shrine to the Penates in their homes, offering them daily prayers and sacrifices. This ensured the family's well-being and protected them from misfortune.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Epic poem detailing Aeneas' journey and the founding of Rome, including the role of the Penates.
sources.text: Epic poem detailing Aeneas' journey and the founding of Rome, including the role of the Penates.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: Historical account of Rome, mentioning the public Penates and their importance to the state.
sources.text: Historical account of Rome, mentioning the public Penates and their importance to the state.

sources.author: Varro
